Timeline

South America: A Full Accounting

c. 12,500 BCE → Present (2026) · top = earliest, bottom = now

Every era of the continent — from the first hearths at Monte Verde to the present day. Standard milestones sit alongside events often minimized in traditional tellings: Indigenous civilizations and their conquest, slavery and its afterlives, caudillos, coups, and foreign interventions. Each entry aims for the documented record, without celebration or condemnation. Click any event for detail; select a country below to view its timeline.

Select a country to view its timeline:
c. 12,500 BCE — first known people on the continent
Present — 2026
Created by Johnnie Banks · Descriptions reflect the documented historical record
